diagrams.title: Strongly Connected vs Weakly Connected Digraph
diagrams.description: Comparison showing strongly connected (path both directions) vs weakly connected (only underlying undirected graph connected)
elements: Directed edges forming strongly connected component
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the difference between strong and weak connectivity

diagrams.title: Types of Digraphs Comparison
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of complete digraph, tournament, regular digraph, and DAG
elements: Complete digraph with all bidirectional edges
Tournament with single direction per pair
diagrams.purpose: Visual distinction between different digraph types

diagrams.title: Adjacency Matrix for Digraph
diagrams.description: Shows how a digraph translates to its adjacency matrix representation
diagrams.purpose: Understanding matrix representation of directed graphs

diagrams.title: Strongly Connected Components Decomposition
diagrams.description: Shows how a digraph is decomposed into SCCs and condensed into a DAG
